name: Neuromorphic Computing
notes: Brain-inspired chips that dramatically reduce energy consumption for AI workloads; addresses AI's growing power constraint. Highly relevant for edge AI and autonomous systems in smart cities where energy efficiency is critical.

name: Sovereign AI Platforms
notes: Nation-specific AI infrastructure stacks including domestic chips, cloud, and models. Aligns with Qatar's knowledge economy goals and need for localized, secure AI capabilities reducing foreign dependency.

name: Generative Watermarking & Provenance
notes: Cryptographic techniques to authenticate AI-generated content and track digital assets. Essential for trust in AI-driven media and verifying authenticity of synthetic media in smart city communications.

name: Epigenome Editing
notes: Advanced CRISPR technologies controlling gene expression without DNA cutting; shows promise for chronic diseases. Aligns with Qatar's healthcare research priorities in precision medicine.

name: Agentic AI Security
notes: Autonomous AI agents that defend against AI-driven cyber threats; early-stage category attracting proven cybersecurity founders. Directly supports QMIC's IoT and connected systems security research.

name: Autonomous Biochemical Sensing
notes: Self-operating sensors for real-time chemical and biological detection in environmental and medical contexts. Relevant for Qatar's environmental monitoring and occupational safety applications.

name: Causal AI
notes: AI systems that understand cause-and-effect relationships rather than correlations. Enables more robust decision-making for smart city operations and transportation systems.

name: Nanozymes
notes: Engineered nanomaterials with enzyme-like activities for medical and environmental applications. Potential for low-cost diagnostics and pollution treatment in urban environments.

name: AI Scientists (Self-Driving Labs)
notes: AI platforms that automate hypothesis generation, experimentation, and analysis to accelerate R&D cycles. Supports Qatar's research and knowledge economy objectives.

name: Shared Autonomous Mobility (Robotaxis)
notes: Fully autonomous ride-hailing services moving from pilots to scaled deployment; regulatory and safety validation ongoing. Core to QMIC's mobility innovation research agenda.

name: AI-Augmented Software Engineering
notes: AI coding agents that generate, test, and debug software; reducing development time but introducing new security and cost management challenges. Accelerates QMIC's software prototyping capabilities.

name: Commercial Space & Defense AI
notes: AI-enabled space systems and defense applications entering mass production; rapid cost reduction driving new use cases. At the intersection of national security and emerging technology.

name: Agentic AI ROI Measurement Platforms
notes: Tools for quantifying business value and productivity gains from AI agent deployments; solving the ROI measurement challenge. Critical for justifying AI investments in public sector smart city initiatives.

name: Digital Twin of the Customer
notes: Dynamic virtual models of individual customer behavior and preferences; hype has faded amid privacy concerns and accuracy limitations. Applicable to personalized mobility services and citizen engagement.

name: Superapps
notes: Integrated platforms combining messaging, payments, transport, and government services; proven in Asia but Western adoption lagging. Aligns with Qatar's digital government and unified service delivery goals.

name: Generative AI for Code & Content
notes: Mature generative models for code, images, and text moving past peak hype; integration challenges and governance concerns persist. Core enabler for digital content and software development in Qatar's knowledge economy.

name: Decentralized Identity (DID)
notes: Self-sovereign digital identity systems; standards exist but adoption remains slow due to user experience and governance challenges. Directly supports QMIC's secure mobility and smart city infrastructure research.

name: Osmotic Energy Systems
notes: Generating clean energy from salinity gradients between salt and fresh water; emerging as viable renewable source. Aligns with Qatar's sustainability and energy diversification objectives.

name: Robotic Swarm Coordination
notes: Multiple robots collaborating for logistics, inspection, agriculture, and infrastructure maintenance. Core to QMIC's embodied intelligent systems research for smart city operations.

name: GLP-1 Therapies (Expanded Applications)
notes: Weight-loss and metabolic drugs showing promise for neurodegenerative and cardiovascular diseases; moving beyond initial indications. Healthcare applications with potential for chronic disease management in Qatar.

name: Structural Battery Composites
notes: Materials that simultaneously store energy and bear structural loads; reducing weight in EVs, drones, and robotics. Enables lighter autonomous vehicles and mobile robots for QMIC's mobility applications.

name: Small Modular Reactors (SMRs)
notes: Scalable, factory-built nuclear reactors; gaining traction for data center and industrial power needs. Long-term energy security option aligning with Qatar's infrastructure planning.

name: Collaborative Sensing Networks
notes: Distributed sensors sharing data via AI for comprehensive environmental and infrastructure monitoring. Directly supports QMIC's smart city, traffic management, and environmental sensing R&D.

name: Green Nitrogen Fixation
notes: Low-energy electrochemical and biological methods for fertilizer production; reducing agriculture's carbon footprint. Supports Qatar's food security and sustainability initiatives.

name: Cloud Data Ecosystems
notes: Integrated data sharing and governance across organizational boundaries; enabling collaborative AI. Applicable to Qatar's smart city data sharing between government and transportation entities.

name: Embodied AI & Physical World Understanding
notes: AI systems that understand and interact with physical environments; powering autonomous navigation and manipulation. Core to QMIC's embodied intelligent systems strategy for robotics and smart city applications.

name: Edge AI & Federated Learning
notes: AI processing at the edge with privacy-preserving distributed training; widely deployed for IoT and real-time analytics. Enables low-latency, privacy-aware smart city services and autonomous systems.

name: Cybersecurity Mesh
notes: Modular, distributed security architecture for identity and access management; mature and widely adopted. Critical for securing Qatar's connected mobility and smart city infrastructure.

name: Humanoid Robots (General Purpose)
notes: Bipedal robots designed for human environments with advanced manipulation; commercial deployments beginning across multiple industries. Aligns with QMIC's advanced robotics research for service and industrial applications.

name: AI for Climate Resilience
notes: AI systems for climate impact prediction, urban adaptation planning, and resource optimization. Directly supports Qatar's environmental sustainability and climate adaptation strategies.
